g.text: The Ultimatum Game
A Simple Game of Cooperation
Definition
The Ultimatum Game is a two-player game where one player (Proposer) is given a sum of money and must divide it between themselves and another player (Reserrer).
Game Mechanics

Initial Setup: The game begins with a sum of money, typically denoted as `x`. The Proposer proposes a division of the money, specifying two numbers: `a` and `b`, where `a` is the amount the Proposer will receive and `b` is the amount the Reserrer will receive.
Proposer's Turn
The Proposer proposes a division of the money, specifying two numbers: `a` and `b`, where `a` is the amount the Proposer will receive and `b` is the amount the Reserrer will receive.
Reserrer's Turn

The Reserrer receives the proposal and has two options: accept or reject the proposal. If the Reserrer accepts, the game ends, and both players receive their allocated shares. If the Reserrer rejects, the game ends, and neither player receives anything.
Acceptance
The Reserrer accepts the proposal and receives the allocated amount `b`.
Rejection
The Reserrer rejects the proposal and receives nothing.
Payoff
The amount each player receives at the end of the game is denoted as `a` and `b`, where `a` is the amount the Proposer will receive and `b` is the amount the Reserrer will receive.
Key Concepts
Proposer: The player who proposes the division of the money.
Reserrer: The player who receives the proposal and has the final say.
Acceptance: When the Reserrer accepts the proposal.
Rejection: When the Reserrer rejects the proposal.
Conclusion
The Ultimatum Game is a simple, yet powerful concept in game theory that has far-reaching implications in economics, sociology, and psychology.
